behavior_summary: Controls NPC 'Beren' gameplay behavior: reactions to spell-casting, theft, player 'look' and 'use' interactions, timed scheduling, and fast-area triggers.
BEREN::cast dispatches either local guards (CALGUARD::0080) when on a specific map (0x29 / 41) or sets a global anger state (berenMad) and invokes spell-combat handling (SPELCMBT::084D) otherwise.

BEREN::AvatarStoleSomething implements an escalating theft-response: validate vertical range (Item::getZ), free stored references (FREE::2A56), warn the player (Item::bark) while incrementing berenWarned, apply stasis (setAvatarInStasis), set Beren's target (Npc::setTarget) and spawn guard/movement/combat routines to engage the thief when warnings escalate.
BEREN::look composes contextual description barks based on berenName, berenJob, and Npc::isDead to present appropriate flavor text.
BEREN::schedule toggles/maintains berenSchedule and launches timed behavior methods (METHOD::143A / METHOD::133F / METHOD::0BCF) depending on map and pyrosFree.
Fast-area handlers (enterFastArea, leaveFastArea) set up immediate targeting or movement toward the avatar and perform cleanup (FREE::2A56) when the avatar leaves.
notable_control_flow: Map-conditional branching: many behaviors branch on Npc::getMap() (notably BEREN::cast and theft responses), producing different spawns and flag writes depending on location.
Escalation loop in BEREN::AvatarStoleSomething uses a berenWarned counter to change outputs and actions (warnings → stasis → setTarget → spawn guards → attack).

Spawn-and-suspend pattern: multiple handlers spawn external/process routines (CALGUARD::0080, SPELCMBT::084D, METHOD::* , MOVE::0080) and then suspend until those processes complete, ensuring synchronous reaction sequences.
Fast-area entry triggers immediate short-circuit behavior (setTarget or spawn MOVE) when certain globals (avatarMasterSorcerer, inDaemonScene) and map conditions are met.
open_questions: Exact numeric semantics of the uword parameter to BEREN::cast — inferred as a spell id or caster action code but not provable from disassembly alone.

Precise effects and parameter contracts of spawned METHOD::* routines (METHOD::091C, METHOD::0BCF, METHOD::143A, METHOD::133F) — their internal behaviors are inferred (movement, timed behavior, scheduler) but not confirmed.
Meaning of some numeric constants pushed into schedule() spawn calls and exact timing semantics used to set berenSchedule.
Whether some global flags (pyrosFree, avatarMasterSorcerer) are shared game-wide or are scene-specific toggles in this mod's environment.

main_function.name: U8P_BEREN::cast
main_function.purpose: Handle Beren being the source of a 'U8P_BEREN::cast' event: choose a location-appropriate reaction (spawn guards when on map 0x29, otherwise mark Beren angry and route to spell combat), coordinate spawned processes and wait for their completion.
entry_conditions: Event 11 (U8P_BEREN::cast) delivered by the engine to Beren
Called with a uword parameter (likely a spell id / action code) pushed by the event dispatch

function_map.name: U8P_BEREN::cast
function_map.role: Entry for handling U8P_BEREN::cast/spell events for Beren; decides guard vs spell-combat behavior and sets 'berenMad' when appropriate.

function_map.name: U8P_BEREN::AvatarStoleSomething
function_map.role: Respond to avatar/item theft: validates theft, issues escalating warnings using berenWarned, applies stasis, sets targets, and spawns guard/movement/combat processes.

function_map.name: U8P_BEREN::look
function_map.role: Produce descriptive bark text when the player 'looks' at Beren using berenName/berenJob and Npc::isDead.

function_map.name: U8P_BEREN::schedule
function_map.role: Periodic scheduler toggling berenSchedule and spawning timed behavior methods according to map and pyrosFree.

function_map.name: U8P_BEREN::enterFastArea
function_map.role: Fast-area entry trigger: may set Beren's target to the avatar or spawn MOVE to approach depending on avatarMasterSorcerer, inDaemonScene and map checks.

function_map.name: U8P_BEREN::leaveFastArea
function_map.role: Fast-area exit trigger: cleanup/unregister a stored reference via FREE::2A56.

function_map.name: U8P_BEREN::use
function_map.role: Handle player 'use' interactions: different behavior when dead vs alive, toggles gumps, and spawns contextual reactions based on berenMad and acolyteKilled.

function_map.name: Npc::getMap
function_map.role: Engine function returning NPC's current map id; used to branch location-specific behavior.

function_map.name: CALGUARD::0080
function_map.role: Spawned guard/attack routine used to dispatch guards in response to casts or theft.

function_map.name: SPELCMBT::084D
function_map.role: Spell-combat handler invoked for off-map casts to resolve spell effects/combats.

internal_calls.purpose: When Npc::getMap() == 0x29 spawn guard routine to respond to the U8P_BEREN::cast and suspend until it finishes.
When not on map 0x29, set berenMad and spawn spell-combat resolution passing the original uword parameter, then suspend until completion.
Set Beren's combat/interest target to the avatar when theft is detected or escalates.
After computing direction with Item::getDirToItem, spawn a movement/attack method toward the stolen item/actor.
When conditions met (not inDaemonScene and map match), approach the avatar by spawning MOVE::0080.

external_calls.purpose: Query Beren's map id to decide whether to spawn CALGUARD or SPELCMBT.
Spawned as the guard response when U8P_BEREN::cast happens on map 0x29.
Spawned to handle spell-combat when U8P_BEREN::cast occurs off-map; receives pid and the uword param.
Check Z coordinate of item to validate theft vertical range before escalating.
Free or unregister a stored reference related to the theft (cleanup) before pursuing escalation actions.
Compute direction from Beren to the item/actor to pass to a movement/attack method.
Temporarily freeze the avatar as part of a theft punishment/escalation.
Emit warning/threat text to the player; used multiple times with escalating messages determined by berenWarned.
Read item/NPC status bits to decide whether to open/close a gump (UI) when player uses Beren.
Open a UI gump for interacting with Beren when conditions match.
Close Beren's UI gump when the use event indicates it should.

reads.name: pyrosFree
reads.likely_effect: Controls which scheduled behavior schedule() will run; read to pick between METHOD spawn variants.

reads.name: avatarMasterSorcerer
reads.likely_effect: If set, causes enterFastArea to immediately set Beren's target to the avatar (gives avatar privileged status).

reads.name: inDaemonScene
reads.likely_effect: Suppresses certain fast-area behaviors when true (enterFastArea short-circuits movement in daemon scenes).

reads.name: berenMad
reads.likely_effect: Read by use() and possibly other behaviors to change Beren's reactions; set by U8P_BEREN::cast when casting off the protected map.

reads.name: acolyteKilled
reads.likely_effect: Read by use() to select alternate interaction sequences (different spawn path when an acolyte is dead).

reads.name: berenName
reads.likely_effect: Determines whether 'look' includes the NPC's proper name in the descriptive text.

reads.name: berenJob
reads.likely_effect: Determines whether 'look' includes job/title information (Sorcerer/Mage) in the description.

reads.name: berenWarned
reads.likely_effect: Tracks how many theft warnings have been issued; read to choose escalation branch in AvatarStoleSomething.

reads.name: berenSchedule
reads.likely_effect: Indicates the current scheduled behavior state for Beren; read/checked and updated by schedule().

writes.name: berenMad
writes.likely_effect: Set (written to a value like 3) when BEREN::cast runs off the protected map to mark Beren as angry and alter future interactions.

writes.name: berenWarned
writes.likely_effect: Incremented on theft incidents to record how many warnings the player has received and drive escalation.

writes.name: berenSchedule
writes.likely_effect: Toggled/updated by schedule() to reflect which timed sequence is active or to clear that state.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Controls NPC 'Beren' gameplay behavior: reactions to spell-casting, theft, player 'look' and 'use' interactions, timed scheduling, and fast-area triggers.
